items.hook: The urban area is densely populated and contains many informal homes.
items.body: The Brown's Farm area is a neighborhood located within the Philippi area of the Cape Flats region of the City of Cape Town, in the Western Cape province of South Africa. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Gracie Abrams captures pain of early adulthood in new album - but you're left wanting more
items.body: The singer's third album is full of incisive, witty lyrics that are let down by muted production.

items.hook: Battle of Alarcos: Almohad forces defeat the Castilian army of Alfonso VIII and force its retreat to Toledo.
items.body: Battle of Alarcos, was fought between the Almohads led by Abu Yusuf Ya'qub al-Mansur and King Alfonso VIII of Castile. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The California Cadet Corps (CACC), originally formed as the California High School Cadets, is a component of the California Military Department's Youth and Community Programs Task Force.
items.body: The California Cadet Corps is an educational leadership youth organization in California open to college students as well as students in high school, middle school, and elementary school. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Government of Detroit, with up to $20 billion in debt, files for the largest municipal bankruptcy in U.S. history.
items.body: The government of Detroit, Michigan mayor, the nine-member Detroit City Council, the eleven-member Board of Police Commissioners, and a clerk. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Germans test fly the Messerschmitt Me 262 using its jet engines for the first time.
items.body: The Messerschmitt Me 262, nicknamed Schwalbe in fighter versions, or Sturmvogel in fighter-bomber versions, is a fighter aircraft and fighter-bomber that was designed and produced by the German aircraft manufacturer Messerschmitt. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Richard Carvel is a historical novel by the American novelist Winston Churchill.
items.body: It was first published in 1899 and was exceptionally successful, selling around two million copies and making the author a rich man. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Roman–Persian Wars: Emperor Julian arrives at Antioch with a Roman expeditionary force (60,000 men) and stays there for nine months to launch a campaign against the Persian Empire.
items.body: The Roman–Persian wars, also called the Roman–Iranian wars, took place between the Greco-Roman world and the Iranian world, beginning with the Roman Republic and the Parthian Empire in 54 BC and ending with the Byzantine and the Sasanian empires in 628 AD. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Lamiel is an unfinished novel by Stendhal set early in the July Monarchy.
items.body: He began writing a draft of it in 1839 and was still working on it at the time of his death in 1842. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Sinister Street is a 1913–1914 novel by Compton Mackenzie.
items.body: It is a kind of Bildungsroman, or novel about growing up, and concerns two children, Michael Fane and his sister Stella. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Sea Devils is a 1953 historical adventure film directed by Raoul Walsh and starring Rock Hudson, Yvonne De Carlo, and Maxwell Reed.
items.body: The story is based on Victor Hugo's novel Toilers of the Sea, which was the working title of the film. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Deception is a 2021 French drama film, directed by Arnaud Desplechin, from a screenplay by Desplechin and Julie Peyr.
items.body: It is based upon the novel of the same name by Philip Roth. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Battle of the Cremera as part of the Roman–Etruscan Wars. Veii ambushes and defeats the Roman army.
items.body: The Battle of the Cremera was fought between the Roman Republic and the Etruscan city of Veii, in 477 BC. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: A Canadair CL-44 and Sukhoi Su-15 collide in mid-air near Yerevan, Armenia, killing four.
items.body: The Canadair CL-44 was a Canadian turboprop airliner and cargo aircraft based on the Bristol Britannia that was developed and produced by Canadair in the late 1950s and early 1960s. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
